page: Amazon Web Services x8g.4xlarge is a memory-optimized server instance powered by custom AWS Graviton Neoverse-V2 processors. Built on the 64-bit ARM architecture, this instance features 16 dedicated physical cores running at 2.8 GHz with one thread per core. It is equipped with 256.0 GB of RAM, yielding a high memory density of 16.0 GB per core. The processor includes 64 KB of L1 data cache, 2 MB of L2 cache, and 36 MB of L3 cache, alongside hardware-accelerated AES cryptography. It operates on the AWS Nitro hypervisor, has a baseline network bandwidth of 8 Gbps, and does not include local storage or dedicated GPU hardware.

Benchmark data highlights the instance's memory subsystem, with the Passmark memory score ranking in the top tier. Large block uncached read memory bandwidth reaches 292.71 GB/sec, and write bandwidth reaches 124.24 GB/sec, both placing in the top 25%. Single-threaded gzip compression and Geekbench text processing also demonstrate strong performance in the top 25%. However, compute-heavy tasks such as LLM inference show limitations, with text generation on a small 135M model scoring in the bottom 10% and prompt processing on a medium 7B model in the bottom 25%.

Given its high memory-to-vCPU ratio and strong memory bandwidth, the x8g.4xlarge provides qualitative cost efficiency for memory-bound workloads that do not require local NVMe storage or high-performance GPU acceleration. It is highly suited for in-memory databases, caching layers, large-scale text processing, and database operations, where its strong memory performance can be fully leveraged.
description: Amazon Web Services x8g.4xlarge is a memory-optimized server instance powered by 16 dedicated ARM64 AWS Graviton Neoverse-V2 physical cores running at 2.8 GHz. Operating on the AWS Nitro hypervisor, it features 256.0 GB of RAM, providing a high memory-to-core ratio of 16.0 GB per core, but lacks local storage and GPU accelerators. Performance benchmarks indicate a top-tier memory subsystem, with Passmark memory scores in the top 10% and large-block memory bandwidth in the top 25%. Conversely, LLM inference tasks show weaker performance, with text generation on small models landing in the bottom 10%. This instance is qualitatively cost-efficient for memory-intensive applications, making it highly suitable for in-memory databases, caching layers, and large-scale text processing workloads.
og_description: Amazon Web Services x8g.4xlarge is a memory-optimized ARM64 server with 16 dedicated Graviton cores and 256 GB RAM. It features top-tier memory performance for databases and caching.
meta_description: Amazon Web Services x8g.4xlarge is an ARM64 memory-optimized server featuring 16 dedicated Graviton cores, 256 GB RAM, and 8 Gbps network bandwidth.
tagline: An ARM-based memory-optimized instance featuring high memory bandwidth and dedicated cores for memory-intensive database and caching workloads.
